stem: Which of the following is NOT a feature of a project?
options: Constrained by limited resources
Planned, executed and controlled
Creates unique product or service
May be ongoing and repetitive
explanation: Operations and projects differ primarily in that operations are ongoing and repetitive while projects are temporary and unique. A project is a temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product or service.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In an automobile company for which you are the project manager, the allowable standard deviation for a product required from the contractor is within 0.002 inches. While examining the product, you find that the standard deviation is slightly more than 0.002 inches. You believe the deviation is very small and hence acceptable. What must you do?
options: Allow the product because it is a very small deviation from the company standard which you think is acceptable
Document the lower quality level, ask the contractor for an explanation, and try to find a solution
Reject the product outright
Allow the lower standard this time but inform the contractor to be more quality conscious going forward
explanation: Any decrease in quality level should be documented and discussed with the contractor. Rejecting outright is too harsh; allowing lower quality without documentation is not advisable.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: All these statements about the project charter are correct EXCEPT:
options: Project charter includes or references the business need and product description
Project charter provides authority to the project manager
Project charter formally authorizes a project
Project charter is issued by the project sponsor
explanation: The project charter should be issued by a manager external to the project, and at a level appropriate to the needs of the project — not necessarily the project sponsor.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: All the following are tools for Estimate Costs EXCEPT:
options: Analogous Estimating
Parametric Modeling
Computerized tools
Alternatives Identification
explanation: Alternatives Identification is a tool for Plan Human Resource Management, not Estimate Costs.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: All the following are tools and techniques for Plan Human Resource Management EXCEPT:
options: Matrix
Networking
Organizational Theory
Recognition and rewards
explanation: Recognition and rewards is a tool for Develop Project Team, not Plan Human Resource Management.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your project, you are creating a diagram that describes the decision under consideration and the implications of choosing one or another of the available alternatives. This will help in:
options: Getting a qualitative analysis of the risk
Determining which risks can impact the project the most
Translating the uncertainties at a detailed level into potential impact on objectives expressed at the level of the total project
Determining which decision yields the greatest expected value
explanation: A Decision Tree is a diagram that describes a decision under consideration and the implications of choosing one or another of the available alternatives. It incorporates probabilities of risks and costs or rewards of each logical path, indicating which decision yields the greatest expected value.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: You are considering whether to buy or make a software product. If you buy, the cost is $80,000 and the cost of procurement and integration is $1,000. If you build, it requires 7 software engineers working 3 months at $4,000/month each, with overhead costs of $2,000. Which option will you choose?
options: Buy
Build
Neither build nor buy
Need more information about the suppliers before making a decision
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your project, you have designated a group of people to monitor changes to the project baselines, manage the approvals, and do the necessary paperwork. These are examples of:
options: Configuration Management
Change Control System
Risk management
Project plan execution
explanation: A Change Control System is a collection of formal, documented procedures that defines how project performance will be monitored and evaluated, including paperwork, tracking systems, processes, and approval levels necessary for authorizing changes.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: When referring to inputs for schedule development, responsibility (i.e. who will perform the work), activity type (i.e. summary or detailed), and WBS classification are part of:
options: Constraints
Activity Attributes
Resource Pool Description
Activity Duration Estimates
explanation: Activity Attributes include responsibility, geographic area, activity type, and WBS classification — important for further selection and sorting of planned activities.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: For Quality Planning, you are using Design of Experiments to determine which factors might influence specific variables. Which of the following is a variable (not a measure)?
options: Dollars
Kilograms
Weight
Pounds
explanation: Weight is a variable. Dollars, Kilograms, and Pounds are all units of measure, not variables.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your last project status meeting, you had 15 team members. The meeting was very disorderly with too many sidebar conversations and arguments. In order to better regulate the meeting next time, you should:
options: Decrease the number of people in one meeting
Publish a meeting agenda
Ensure that you control the channels of communication
Give incentives to team members for conforming to desired norms of the meeting
explanation: Publishing a meeting agenda is the best way to ensure meetings are conducted in an orderly fashion. Controlling all communication channels is not feasible — for N people there are N×(N-1) channels.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In a Weak Matrix organization, the role of a project manager is that of a:
options: Coordinator and expeditor
Project manager with considerable authority
Support person
Functional manager
explanation: Weak matrix organizations maintain many characteristics of a functional organization, and the project manager role is more that of a coordinator or expediter than that of a manager.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your new company, you realize that some financial statements do not follow US GAAP Accounting Standards and are hence inaccurate. What should you do first?
options: Inform the US government agencies of the inaccurate accounting practices in the company
Talk to your finance managers about why you think they may be violating the standard accounting standards
Since you are new to the company, assume that finance standards are different and do not do anything
Talk with a finance manager in your previous company to get expert opinion
explanation: Talking to the finance managers first aims at understanding and resolving the problem internally. Going to government agencies is a last resort. Talking to previous employer's finance manager risks compromising proprietary information.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your project, a team member suggested the addition of a functionality to improve customer satisfaction. Your approach would be to:
options: Allow for the functionality because satisfying the customer is your objective
Do not allow for the new requirement, because it will be a change in scope, and the customer did not explicitly mention the requirement
Make changes to the project plan to accommodate the new requirement
Ask the customer for additional funding for implementing the requirement
explanation: A project must prevent gold plating — adding requirements not explicitly stated in the project scope. Define Scope ensures the project includes all the work required, and only the work required.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In your construction project, the CPI is 1.30 and SPI is 0.85. What could be the potential reason?
options: A critical resource went on sick leave for a long period of time, and this had not been anticipated earlier
The cost of raw materials required for construction increased 10%
You had not taken into account the inflation rate
There were 4 days of waiting time in the curing of cement, and no work could be done during that time
explanation: Options 2 and 3 increase costs — but CPI > 1 means cost is fine. Option 4 was a known factor that should have been planned for. Only option 1 could cause schedule delay (SPI < 1) without affecting cost performance.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Team Development is a very critical component of Develop Project Team. All the following are inputs EXCEPT:
options: Resource Calendars
Project Staff Assignments
Work Performance Reports
Human Resource Management Plan
explanation: Work Performance Reports is an input to Manage Project Team, not Develop Project Team.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: You have just completed Qualitative Risk Analysis of the project. What should you be doing next?
options: Sensitivity Analysis
Risk Probability and Impact
Data Precision Ranking
Brainstorming
explanation: Risk Probability and Impact and Data Precision Ranking are part of Qualitative Analysis (already done). Brainstorming is part of Identify Risks (already done). Sensitivity Analysis is the next step in Quantitative Risk Management.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: If a project needs to be terminated early, then you will have to do:
options: Procurement Planning
Contract Administration
Procurement Audits
Source Selection
explanation: Procurement Audits are needed to capture procurement lessons learned, which is essential when a project is terminated early.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: You are the Project Manager for a company doing research on alternative fuels for automobiles. Which of the following is a valid assumption?
options: Cost of the project is $5 Million
The project scope is to develop alternative fuels only for automobile vehicles weighing less than 10 tons
Gasoline fuels will become very expensive and less readily available after 10 years
The project has to be completed within 2 years
explanation: Assumptions are factors considered to be true without proof. Options 1, 2, and 4 are constraints (applicable restrictions). Only option 3 is an assumption — a future belief treated as true for planning purposes.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Your project will most likely be completed in 10 weeks. Worst case scenario, the project will require 19 weeks, and if everything goes well it will be completed in 7 weeks. What is the PERT estimate for the task?
options: 10 weeks
11 weeks
9 weeks
Cannot be determined with the information provided
explanation: PERT estimate = (Optimistic + 4×Most Likely + Pessimistic) / 6 = (7 + 4×10 + 19) / 6 = 66 / 6 = 11 weeks.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Control Quality involves monitoring specific project results to determine if they comply with relevant quality standards. To evaluate Control Quality outputs, the project team should have a working knowledge of:
options: Prevention and Inspection
Sampling and Probability
Special Causes and Random Causes
Tolerances and Control Limits
explanation: The project management team must have a working knowledge of statistical quality control, especially sampling and probability, to help evaluate quality control outputs.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In a sender-receiver model, all the following are true EXCEPT:
options: Sender is responsible for making information clear, unambiguous and complete
Sender confirms that information is understood
Receiver is responsible for making sure that the information is completely received and understood
All the statements above are true
explanation: All three statements are actually correct: the sender is responsible for clear, unambiguous, complete information and for confirming it is understood; the receiver is responsible for making sure information is received entirely and understood correctly. So 'All statements are true' is actually the correct answer here — the EXCEPT refers to finding the false statement, and there is none.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Preparing the Financial plan for your project will require:
options: Project Management skills
Industry specific skills
Financial Subject Matter Expertise
Both Project Management and Industry specific skills
explanation: Preparing a financial plan requires both Project Management skills and Industry specific skills working together.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: All the following are responsibilities of a certified PMP EXCEPT:
options: Ensure that there is no conflict of interest that can compromise the legitimate interests of a client or customer
Ensure that the technical specifications are appropriately defined
Disseminate PMP Code of Professional Conduct to other PMI certificants
Cooperate with PMI concerning ethics violations and the collection of related information
explanation: The project manager may not be a technical expert to determine the accuracy of technical specifications. All others are mentioned in the PMP Code of Professional Conduct as responsibilities of the project manager.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) is:
options: Developed in the Scope Planning Phase
A deliverable-oriented grouping of project components
Similar to the chart of accounts
Depicts work elements assigned to different organizational units
explanation: WBS is a deliverable-oriented grouping of project components that organizes and defines the total scope of the project. Work not in the WBS is out of scope.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: In a project you are presented with four options. Which project should you select?
options: Project A with Opportunity Cost of $100,000
Project B with Benefit-Cost Ratio of 0.75
Project C with IRR of -2%
Project D with NPV of $100,000
explanation: Project B (BCR < 1) and Project C (negative IRR) result in losses. Opportunity Cost is not a project selection criterion. Project D with a positive NPV of $100,000 is the best choice.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: What is the factor which will help a project manager gain the maximum support of the assigned project personnel?
options: Expertise of the project manager
Work Challenge
Ability of the project manager to penalize team members
Ability of project manager to provide promotions to team members
explanation: Expertise of the project manager is the most important factor in getting support from project personnel. The ability to penalize is actually the least important factor.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: The Risk Management Plan may include:
options: Budgeting, Timing, Thresholds, Methodology
Risks, Triggers, Inputs to other processes
Avoided, Transferred, Mitigated and Accepted Risks
Risk Response Audits, Earned Value Analysis, Technical Performance Measurement
explanation: The Risk Management Plan includes budgeting (resources for risk activities), timing (when risk processes will be performed), thresholds, and methodology. Options 2 and 3 describe the Risk Register. Option 4 describes tools for risk monitoring.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: Procurement Negotiations is carried out as part of:
options: Solicitation Planning
Solicitation
Conduct Procurements
Contract Administration
explanation: Procurement Negotiations take place during Conduct Procurements, where the buyer and seller negotiate the final contract terms and conditions.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: You are the Project Manager for a construction project. Your job requires that you sanction work to others and ensure that the work is done at the right time. The tool you use is:
options: Work Authorization System
Project Scheduling Techniques
Project Plan
Network Diagram
explanation: A Work Authorization System is a formal procedure for sanctioning project work to ensure that work is done at the right time and in the proper sequence.
source: Projex Academy – PMP Practice Questions

stem: A company is considering two projects, Alpha and Beta. Project Alpha is expected to result in a $50 million net profit, while project Beta is expected to net $45 million. The financial controller has stated that the company can only invest in one of these projects. If project Alpha is selected, what will be the opportunity cost?
options: $95 million
$50 million
$45 million
$5 million
explanation: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative foregone. If Alpha is chosen, the company gives up Beta's $45 million profit.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110002)

stem: You are managing a project to build five bridges. The project consists of five sequential phases with each phase delivering one bridge. After the first phase of the project is complete, which process group of the second phase should follow?
options: Initiating
Planning
Executing
Closing
explanation: Each project phase begins with the Initiating process group to formally authorize the phase and ensure alignment before planning begins.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110003)

stem: The project aims to investigate the feasibility of constructing a hydroelectric dam from a technical, economic, and social point of view. The project charter has been approved. Which of the following processes should be performed next?
options: Develop Project Management Plan
Identify Risks
Identify Stakeholders
Develop Project Charter
explanation: After the project charter is approved, the next step is to Identify Stakeholders, as stakeholder identification is an early Initiating process group activity.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110005)

stem: You are managing a software upgrade project. Your project sponsor has a great deal of authority regarding project decisions, but doesn't have much interest in your project. What stakeholder management strategy should you use with your project sponsor?
options: Keep the sponsor satisfied
Manage the sponsor closely
Monitor the sponsor's actions
Keep the sponsor informed
explanation: High power, low interest stakeholders should be kept satisfied. The power/interest grid prescribes 'keep satisfied' for those with high authority but low engagement.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110006)

stem: The project sponsor asks for a copy of the document that contains the description, owner, source, priority, and status of product requirements. Which project document is the project sponsor requesting?
options: The requirements management plan
The requirements traceability matrix
The scope management plan
The work breakdown structure (WBS)
explanation: The requirements traceability matrix links requirements to their source and tracks attributes such as owner, priority, and status throughout the project.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110008)

stem: You are facing problems decomposing the testing work package because detailed testing plans cannot be determined until the system is at least 50% developed. What is the best way to resolve this problem?
options: Use product analysis techniques such as product breakdown and systems analysis to decompose the system testing work package
Decompose the system development work package now and decompose the system testing work package later
Break down the project into multiple phases so that the system testing work package goes into the second project phase
Consult the project management plan to determine what to do in this situation
explanation: Rolling wave planning allows decomposition of near-term work in detail while future work packages are decomposed as more information becomes available.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110011)

stem: You are about to start planning for a large and complex project. As a starting point for initial project planning, what is the first thing you should do?
options: Conduct a project kick-off meeting to inform and engage stakeholders and gain commitment
Review the project charter to understand the high-level information about the project
Begin the process of identifying stakeholders so they can be engaged as necessary for project planning
Share the project scope statement with the project team for a common understanding of project deliverables
explanation: The project charter is the foundational document that authorizes the project and provides high-level information. Reviewing it is the first step before any planning activities.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110014)

stem: As you are monitoring the cost baseline for your project, you notice that project expenses are higher than expected at this point in the project. What is the most likely cause of the variance?
options: Overestimated material costs
Lack of stakeholder support
An incomplete milestone list
Excess inventory
explanation: Excess inventory ties up capital and increases holding costs, making it a common cause of cost overruns related to resource management.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110020)

stem: Recently, you promoted your star programmer, Sam, to lead the agile development team. Sam was a fantastic software coder, but his performance in his new role is not meeting your expectations. Which empirical rule did you forget while considering the promotion of Sam?
options: Expectancy theory
Halo effect
Pareto concept
Murphy's law
explanation: The halo effect is the tendency to assume that a person who excels in one area will excel in another unrelated area, such as assuming a top coder will be a great team leader.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110029)

stem: You have just taken over a project in execution. Project team members spend a lot of time responding to unexpected stakeholder requests for information, causing the project to fall behind schedule. What should you do first?
options: Tell the team members to stop responding to unexpected requests since it is not the responsibility of the team
Push the team to respond to all the requests faster so that they can perform their assigned project activities on time
Ask the team to forward all stakeholder inquiries to you so that the team members can focus on their assigned tasks
Review the communications management plan to understand how stakeholder requests should be handled
explanation: The communications management plan defines how stakeholder information needs are addressed. Reviewing it first ensures responses align with the approved communication approach.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110032)

stem: A team member submits a draft requirements management plan. The project manager notices one component does not belong there. Which of the following components did the project manager most likely notice?
options: A process that describes how the project requirements will be prioritized
The metrics that will be used and the rationale for using them
A process that specifies how a project scope statement will be prepared
How requirement activities will be planned, tracked, and reported
explanation: The requirements management plan covers how requirements are collected, tracked, and reported. How to prepare the project scope statement belongs in the scope management plan, not the requirements management plan.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110034)

stem: While reviewing your project's performance, you discover a significant variance. A further review reveals that bringing the project back on track requires only a minor correction to a configuration element. What should you do first?
options: Implement the corrective action
Submit a change request
Consult the project sponsor
Delay the customer inspection
explanation: All corrective actions, even minor ones, should go through the formal change control process by submitting a change request before implementation.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110038)

stem: A project manager is performing the Monitor and Control Project Work process. Which of the following activities might be carried out during this process?
options: Comparing actual project performance against the project management plan
Implementing approved change requests to achieve the project's objectives
Analyzing change requests and either approving or rejecting them
Gaining formal acceptance of the deliverables by the customer or sponsor
explanation: Monitor and Control Project Work involves tracking, reviewing, and reporting on project performance against the project management plan to identify variances.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110039)

stem: An organization has a policy that a CCB must review all change requests, and project managers and the project team do not serve as CCB members. Which of the following activities might be performed by members of the CCB?
options: Submitting a change request
Implementing approved change requests
Initiating and closing a project
Monitoring implementation of approved changes
explanation: The CCB reviews and approves or rejects change requests, and monitors the implementation of approved changes. Submitting and implementing changes are done by other parties.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110040)

stem: A company has a dedicated CCB to review all change requests. The project sponsor is a member of the CCB. Which of the following best describes who can request a change on a project?
options: Project manager
Project sponsor
Project team
Any stakeholder
explanation: Any stakeholder can submit a change request. The CCB (of which the sponsor is a member) then reviews and decides on those requests.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110041)

stem: One of your contractors is not performing to agreed-upon standards. You want to terminate the contract and determine the specific legal procedure for early termination. What should you do?
options: Check the procurement management plan
Look at the scope statement
Review the risk register
Consult the contract
explanation: The contract itself contains the terms and conditions for early termination and the legal procedures to follow. It is the authoritative document for this purpose.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110055)

stem: A project is divided into four sequential phases. The second phase has just been completed and the third phase is about to begin. What should the project manager do first?
options: Begin the process of directing and managing the project work
Perform the process of identifying the project stakeholders
Start the process of monitoring and controlling the project work
Begin the process of developing the project management plan
explanation: At the start of each new project phase, stakeholders should be identified (or re-identified) as part of the Initiating process group activities.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110058)

stem: A PMO ensures all relevant project documents are created for all projects. What should the project manager review first to identify stakeholders for a newly started project?
options: The business documents as a source of information about the project's stakeholders
The communications management plan to learn about the project's stakeholders
The stakeholder engagement plan to identify the management strategies and actions required to engage stakeholders effectively
The requirements documentation for information on potential stakeholders
explanation: Business documents (business case and benefits management plan) are primary inputs to Identify Stakeholders and provide early information about potential stakeholders.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110061)

stem: A project manager is developing a stakeholder register using brain writing as a data gathering technique. What is the first thing to do when using that technique?
options: Convene a data gathering meeting with the participants
Develop your own template for a stakeholder register
Send a list of questions to participants before the creativity session
Consult the stakeholder engagement plan to understand how the stakeholders are to be identified
explanation: Brain writing involves sending questions to participants before the session so they can independently generate ideas, which are then shared and discussed during the session.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110065)

stem: A project manager uses specific techniques when composing emails to reduce misunderstandings. Which of the following is NOT considered one of these techniques?
options: Concise expression
Clear purpose directed to the needs of the reader
Controlled flow of words and ideas
Communications register
explanation: Effective written communication techniques include concise expression, clear purpose, and controlled flow. A communications register is a project document, not a writing technique.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110066)

stem: You are leading a brainstorming session to identify project risks and immediately proceed to assign risk owners and ask them to develop risk response plans. Have you properly conducted risk management planning?
options: No, you should be the one developing the risk response plans as you are ultimately responsible for the outcomes of the project.
Yes, the risk response plans are an output of the Identify Risks process and should be developed right after the risks have been identified.
No, before assigning risk responsibility to team members, you need to assess each risk's probability of occurrence and its impact.
Yes, the team members should be assigned responsibility for a risk as soon as possible and tasked with creating a risk response plan.
explanation: After identifying risks, qualitative and/or quantitative risk analysis must be performed to assess probability and impact before developing risk responses.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110070)

stem: You are managing a database project. The team is using brainstorming, focus groups, interviews, questionnaires, and benchmarking to understand and document the stakeholders' needs. These methods are representative of which tool and technique?
options: Data gathering
Data analysis
Decision making
Data representation
explanation: Brainstorming, focus groups, interviews, questionnaires, and benchmarking are all data gathering techniques used in the Collect Requirements process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110072)

stem: The project is in execution but your team cannot stop arguing about project milestones and the specific risks associated with them. What would likely have helped avoid this conflict had it been properly created first?
options: A project charter
Physical resource assignments
Quality metrics
A work breakdown structure
explanation: A properly developed WBS defines the scope of work and provides a common understanding of deliverables and milestones, reducing ambiguity and team conflict.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110074)

stem: During project execution, the team discovers some buttons on a remote control do not work according to specifications. A change request is submitted and approved to replace the buttons. Which of the following best describes this change request?
options: Corrective action
Preventive action
Defect repair
Gold plating
explanation: Defect repair is a type of change request used to modify a nonconforming product or product component. Replacing defective buttons addresses a known defect.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110075)

stem: A project manager provides an estimate of 8-10 months based on her experience with three similar projects when a customer asks for a rough estimate. What estimation technique does the project manager use?
options: Expert judgment
Three-point estimating
Analogous estimating
Bottom-up estimating
explanation: Analogous estimating uses historical data from similar projects to estimate the duration or cost of the current project. It is a top-down technique used when limited detail is available.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110076)

stem: A project manager uses a set of technical guidelines applied during the design phase to optimize quality. What tool or technique is the project manager using?
options: Design for X (DfX) where the X in DfX represents the quality aspect of the product
Design of experiments (DOE) as a quality improvement methodology
The Manage Quality process to ensure compliance with project quality requirements
Process analysis to identify opportunities for process improvements
explanation: Design for X (DfX) refers to a set of technical guidelines used during design to optimize specific aspects of the product such as quality, reliability, or manufacturability.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110079)

stem: You are classifying stakeholders with regard to the direction of their influence. You have come to the project sponsor. How should this stakeholder be classified?
options: Downward
Sideward
Outward
Upward
explanation: The project sponsor sits above the project manager in organizational hierarchy. The sponsor's influence flows downward to the project, meaning the project manager interacts upward toward the sponsor.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110081)

stem: During project execution, a senior engineer suggests a simpler and more efficient design that could benefit the project. What should the project manager do next?
options: Nothing, no changes are needed since the project scope was rigorously defined
Discuss the change with the project sponsor
Evaluate the impact of the proposed change on the project constraints
Ask that the engineer submit a change request
explanation: All changes, even beneficial ones, must go through the formal change control process. The first step is to have the initiator submit a change request.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110083)

stem: You are estimating activity durations using a document containing information on categories of labor, material, and equipment, as well as skill levels, required certifications, and grade levels for supplies. Which document would you primarily refer to?
options: Resource breakdown structure
Organizational breakdown structure
Work breakdown structure
Risk breakdown structure
explanation: The resource breakdown structure (RBS) is a hierarchical representation of resources by category and type, including labor, material, equipment, and their required attributes.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110087)

stem: While performing project work, a team member notices a potential new risk that could impact the project's critical path. What should the team member do immediately?
options: Recommend the project be placed on hold for a day to avoid the risk
Document the risk in the risk register
Draft an emergency fallback plan since the critical path is in jeopardy
Generate a risk report using quantitative and qualitative analysis of the identified risk to alert the team of the schedule impacts
explanation: Newly identified risks should be immediately documented in the risk register so they can be assessed and managed through the risk management process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6110090)

stem: You are nearing the end of a project and cannot reach a satisfactory resolution with a vendor through direct conversation. What should you do?
options: Pay the vendor as some products were delivered
Take the vendor to court
Discontinue further meetings with the vendor and hold back payment
Identify some form of alternative dispute resolution
explanation: When direct negotiation fails, the PMBOK Guide recommends alternative dispute resolution (ADR) methods such as mediation or arbitration before pursuing litigation.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112005)

stem: Your company was awarded a contract with a 10% bonus payment if the project is completed two months early. What is the best course of action to increase the chances of receiving the bonus?
options: Reduce project scope
Level resources
Crash the schedule
Purchase insurance
explanation: Crashing the schedule involves adding resources to shorten the project duration, which is the most direct way to complete the project ahead of schedule and earn the bonus.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112009)

stem: The quality management plan specifies the use of a data representation technique to show the strength of relationships between four factors. Which tool or technique should be used?
options: Affinity diagram
Matrix diagram
Scatter diagram
Quality diagram
explanation: A matrix diagram shows the strength of relationships between factors, requirements, or characteristics. It is used to analyze the relationship among multiple factors.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112010)

stem: You have just taken over a project where some deliverables will be produced by external vendors. Where would you look to find information about those involved from the sellers' side?
options: Bid documents
Stakeholder register
Seller proposals
Stakeholder engagement plan
explanation: The stakeholder register is the document that contains information about all identified stakeholders, including external vendors and sellers involved in the project.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112012)

stem: Your project has been plagued with numerous change requests. What is the most likely reason?
options: Incomplete stakeholder register
Lack of a detailed procedure to close the project
Failure to implement risk responses
Change management plan did not authorize a change control board
explanation: An incomplete stakeholder register leads to missing stakeholders and unaddressed needs, which often result in numerous change requests during project execution.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112018)

stem: During the build phase, the customer found some welding joints on the crane were defective. How could this situation have been avoided?
options: Outsourcing the welding to an external vendor
Testing the welding before the customer walkthrough
Using the requirements traceability matrix
Defects are part of any project and cannot be avoided.
explanation: Performing quality control inspections (testing the welding) before the customer walkthrough would have identified and allowed repair of defects prior to delivery.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112019)

stem: A PMO is reviewing a draft benefits management plan and highlights an element to be removed. Which of the following was most likely the element the PMO requested to remove?
options: Expected tangible and intangible value to be gained by the implementation of the project
A set of options to be considered for addressing the business opportunity
A timeframe for realizing the benefits of the project
Measures to be used to show benefits realized
explanation: A set of options for addressing the business opportunity belongs in the business case, not the benefits management plan. The benefits management plan focuses on when and how benefits will be realized.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112028)

stem: To define the product, the project team is using a product analysis technique that helps break down high-level requirements to the level of detail needed to design the product. Which product analysis technique is being used?
options: Work breakdown structure
Product planning
Progressive elaboration
Product breakdown
explanation: Product breakdown is a product analysis technique used during Define Scope that decomposes high-level requirements into detailed product components and specifications.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112039)

stem: A three-phase project to build a prototype is underway. The first phase (design) is now at its end. What should be done with the phase's deliverables?
options: Verified by the testing team for quality compliance
Transferred to the production team
Transitioned to the project management office (PMO)
Handed over to the manufacturing team
explanation: The design phase deliverables (design specifications) are inputs to the next phase. They should be handed over to the manufacturing team who will produce parts in phase two.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112054)

stem: A project nearing its end receives an approved change request to replace a defective unit. What should the project manager do next?
options: Capture the problem in the issue log
Meet with the change control board
Have the defective unit repaired
Have the defective unit replaced
explanation: Since the change request has already been approved, the next step is to implement it — i.e., have the defective unit replaced as directed.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112056)

stem: A technician found a defect and submitted a change request to repair it, indicating significant rework will be required. What should the project manager do first?
options: Approve the change request
Consult the change management plan
Instruct the team to repair the defect
Reject the change request due to significant rework
explanation: The change management plan defines the process for reviewing and approving change requests. The project manager should consult it to follow the correct procedure.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112060)

stem: At the end of a design project, a competitor's new technology rendered the project's deliverable obsolete. The sponsor thinks the project should have been terminated much sooner. What might the project manager have done differently?
options: Used phases and phase gates
Established a change control board (CCB) to review and render decisions for all submitted change requests
Terminated the project when the new technology was introduced
Scheduled more frequent lessons learned meetings with the project team
explanation: Using phases and phase gates allows the organization to assess viability at each gate and make go/no-go decisions, enabling timely termination when the project no longer makes business sense.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112061)

stem: A project to construct a wind farm in an area with no communication infrastructure plans to use project management software for progress monitoring and daily status updates to the sponsor. Which is the most likely challenge the teams will face while controlling the schedule?
options: Stability of requirements
Technology support
Human resource availability
Project importance
explanation: Working in an area with no communication infrastructure means technology support for project management software and daily status updates will be the primary challenge.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112064)

stem: A project is behind schedule because team members were reassigned. After negotiations, you agreed to a temporary solution where a core group of project resources is dedicated until more resources are approved. What conflict resolution technique did you most likely employ?
options: Compromise or reconcile
Force or direct
Collaborate or problem solve
Smooth or accommodate
explanation: Compromise/reconcile involves finding a middle-ground solution where both parties give up something. A temporary partial resource allocation is a compromise between competing demands.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112068)

stem: During a daily stand-up, a programmer mentions she added functionality not in the original design because she had extra time and believes the customer will appreciate it. What is your best course of action?
options: Remove the added functionality and ensure that the project team builds the software as designed going forward
Thank the programmer for the extra effort and notify the customer of the new functionality that has been added
Update the cost baseline since the project will run over budget due to the cost of adding new functionality
Accept the added functionality, but ask the programmer to build only what has been designed going forward
explanation: Adding unauthorized functionality is gold plating and constitutes scope creep. The unapproved feature should be removed and the team reminded to build only what has been approved.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112072)

stem: The project manager wants to determine whether the processes currently in use are effective in meeting the quality requirements specified by the customer. What does the project manager need to obtain first?
options: Quality control measurements
Resource breakdown structure
Requirements traceability matrix
Acceptance of deliverables from the customer
explanation: Quality control measurements are an input to Manage Quality and provide the data needed to evaluate whether processes are producing results that meet quality requirements.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112082)

stem: During inspection, the team detects the same defect that was identified in a previous inspection. The project manager learns the approved change request to repair the defect was never implemented. What could have helped prevent this situation?
options: Holding an approved change requests review
Conducting a retrospective meeting
Performing a root cause analysis
Creating a quality report
explanation: Reviewing approved change requests ensures that all approved changes are tracked and implemented. A regular review of approved change requests would have caught the missing implementation.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112095)

stem: The team completed the Plan Quality Management process and produced the quality management plan plus necessary updates. What did they forget?
options: Determine quality metrics
Define quality roles and responsibilities
Revise the lessons learned register
Update the requirements traceability matrix
explanation: Quality metrics are a key output of the Plan Quality Management process and define how quality will be measured. They are required alongside the quality management plan.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112200)

stem: A client requests to add an outdoor sauna just before project completion. You determine the change would not impact the completion date. What should you do first?
options: Begin construction since the project completion date is not violated
Review the requested change against all project constraints
Have the sponsor verify if the change violates construction regulations
Consult with the change control board on how to handle the change
explanation: Before proceeding with any change, the project manager should evaluate its impact on all project constraints (scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk), not just the completion date.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112235)

stem: You are drafting a project charter for an agile product development project and want to bring stakeholders and SMEs together to discuss perceived risk, success criteria, and other topics. What is the best way to accomplish this?
options: Facilitate an iteration retrospective
Invite the relevant participants to the daily scrum meetings
Conduct interviews with the identified individuals
Schedule a focus group
explanation: A focus group brings together stakeholders and subject matter experts in a structured facilitated discussion to gather input on a specific topic, making it ideal for discussing risk and success criteria.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112294)

stem: A multi-phase project has reached a phase gate where stakeholders want to determine if the phase met its success criteria and if the project should proceed. In addition to the project management plan, which documents will be required?
options: Risk register and risk report
Lessons learned register and lessons learned repository
Business documents and project charter
Agreements including procurement contracts
explanation: Business documents (business case and benefits management plan) and the project charter are key inputs to the phase-end review (Close Project or Phase), as they define original intent and success criteria.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112434)

stem: You are establishing a knowledge management system for your project. Which of the following actions will be the LEAST beneficial in this process?
options: Reviewing the lesson learned register
Monitoring stakeholder engagement
Examining the resource breakdown structure
Studying the project team assignments
explanation: The resource breakdown structure categorizes resources but does not contribute meaningfully to knowledge management. The other options directly relate to identifying knowledge sources and knowledge holders.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112456)

stem: You want to get two new team members up to speed with the overall dynamics of the project and help them engage effectively with stakeholders. What should you do?
options: Ask the team members to record any issues they encounter into the issue log
Review the stakeholder register with the new team members
Schedule a meeting with the new team members and all project stakeholders
Submit a change request to update the stakeholder engagement plan
explanation: The stakeholder register contains key information about each stakeholder's role, interests, and engagement level. Reviewing it with new team members is the most efficient onboarding action.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112463)

stem: During a team charter development meeting, a team member argues the exercise is unnecessary since the team has worked together before. How should you respond?
options: As long as everyone is in agreement, then let's skip the team charter and get ahead of schedule
Let's use the team charter from our last project as a starting point and then update it as necessary
The team charter is required by the project management office (PMO) for all projects
The team charter will eliminate any potential conflicts which will improve our productivity
explanation: The team charter establishes working agreements and norms that prevent conflicts and improve productivity. Its value is in creating shared expectations, regardless of whether the team has worked together before.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112493)

stem: You are preparing a bid package for prospective sellers on an oil pipeline project. Which of the following documents would you leave out of the package?
options: Request for proposal
Statement of work
Independent cost estimates
Bid documents
explanation: Independent cost estimates are used internally by the buyer to evaluate seller proposals; they are not shared with prospective sellers in the bid package.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112596)

stem: You are developing the communications management plan and want to determine information needs of the project stakeholders, focusing on type and format. What is your best course of action?
options: Develop the stakeholder register
Review the requirements documentation
Conduct a communication requirements analysis
Consult the stakeholder engagement plan
explanation: Communication requirements analysis is the technique used in Plan Communications Management to identify stakeholder information needs, including the type, format, and frequency of communications.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112605)

stem: A servant leader assigned to a product development project believes Kanban is well-suited, but the team is unfamiliar with it. What is the servant leader's best course of action?
options: Discuss Kanban during a daily standup meeting
Review Kanban at the iteration retrospective
Let the team members learn Kanban on their own
Have the team attend a workshop on Kanban
explanation: A servant leader supports team growth and capability. Having the team attend a Kanban workshop provides the foundational knowledge needed to effectively adopt the new practice.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112614)

stem: You are developing the project scope statement. You have specified the deliverables, acceptance criteria, and stated what is out of scope. What else should you include?
options: Project exclusions
Acceptance criteria
Project approval requirements
Product scope description
explanation: The product scope description is a key component of the project scope statement that describes the characteristics of the product, service, or result the project will create.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112667)

stem: A project manager wants to understand the availability of team members required to complete project work. What should she do to find this information?
options: Look at the resource calendars
Contact the project management office
Review the resource requirements
Examine the resource breakdown structure
explanation: Resource calendars document the working days, shifts, and availability of specific resources. They are the primary reference for determining when team members are available.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112676)

stem: A project to reduce the defect rate of a manufacturing process is underway. The team is performing quality assurance activities. Which of the following would be most beneficial for this project?
options: Introducing the use of a kanban board into the manufacturing process
Consulting with a certified Six Sigma Black Belt expert
Utilizing rolling wave planning for the project
Adopting a just-in-time (JIT) manufacturing process
explanation: Six Sigma is a quality improvement methodology specifically focused on reducing process defects. A certified Black Belt has the expertise to lead the quality improvement effort.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112685)

stem: During execution, a project experienced several critical risks that were foreseen and previously identified. Despite meticulous planning, the project failed to achieve its objectives due to these realized risks. What may have gone wrong?
options: The realized risks had not been identified earlier in the project
The risk responses were poorly devised
The qualitative risk analysis was inadequate
There was little or no action taken to manage the known risks
explanation: Since risks were identified but the project still failed, the most likely cause is that although responses were planned, they were not executed. Implementing risk responses is as critical as planning them.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112687)

stem: A team member gave a project status presentation that stakeholders found not very engaging. What is the best way for the project manager to address this with the team member?
options: Discuss the issue with the team member
Prepare a formal written document
Utilize formal verbal communication
Send an email to the team member
explanation: For sensitive interpersonal feedback, a direct, informal discussion is most appropriate. It allows for two-way communication, empathy, and coaching.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112688)

stem: A corporate restructuring project has disrupted many key stakeholders' responsibilities, causing engagement challenges. Which of the following actions is the project manager LEAST likely to perform?
options: Manage stakeholder expectations through negotiation and communication
Develop approaches to involve stakeholders based on their needs, expectations, interests, and potential impact on the project
Address any risks or potential concerns related to stakeholder management and anticipate future issues
Engage stakeholders at appropriate project stages to obtain, confirm, or maintain their continued commitment
explanation: During execution, the project manager focuses on managing engagement, not re-planning approaches. Developing new stakeholder involvement approaches belongs in the planning process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112694)

stem: You captured the problem that your leased office space is no longer available and the team cannot extend. What should you do next?
options: Record the issue in the issue log
Revise the resource management plan
Update the risk register
Request additional funds
explanation: The problem (loss of office space) is now an active issue. It should be recorded in the issue log so it can be tracked, assigned, and resolved.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112702)

stem: During a bidder conference, a section is found to be missing from the RFQ. Most prospective suppliers failed to address the missing section in their responses. What is the best course of action?
options: Hold the bidder conference again, and explain the importance of the point that was initially missed
Select from the three vendors that submitted complete proposals
Send a revised RFQ to all prospective suppliers and allow them the opportunity to resubmit their proposals
Extend the deadline and allow all of the vendors to resubmit their proposals
explanation: Fairness in procurement requires that all prospective sellers have the same information. A revised RFQ should be sent to all, giving everyone equal opportunity to respond to the complete requirements.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112760)

stem: You are reprioritizing the product backlog, determining velocity for past iterations, adapting work plans accordingly, and facilitating retrospectives every two weeks. Which process describes the work you are performing?
options: Monitor Risks
Manage Quality
Control Schedule
Develop Schedule
explanation: Control Schedule involves monitoring project status to update the schedule and manage changes. Reprioritizing backlog, measuring velocity, and adapting plans are agile techniques within this process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112783)

stem: You are tailoring the schedule management plan template for an adaptive approach in an organization traditionally using predictive methods. Which components are you likely to tailor the most?
options: The rules of performance measurement, the summary milestones, and the key deliverables
The organizational procedures links, the units of measure, and the project organization charts
The schedule model development, the release and iteration length, and the reporting formats
The project schedule model maintenance, the level of accuracy, and the level of precision
explanation: Switching to an adaptive approach requires significant tailoring of schedule model development (iterative), release/iteration length (sprint cadence), and reporting formats (burndown, velocity).
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112785)

stem: A project team performs monthly risk audits internally. An executive criticizes this, stating only external resources should conduct risk audits. How should the project manager respond?
options: Explain that risk audits can be performed either internally or externally as long as they follow the project management plan
Agree with the executive and submit a change request to update the project management plan to have the audits conducted externally
Follow the guidance provided by the executive and hire a team of external auditors to conduct the risk audits going forward
Explain that if the current risk audits are not sufficient, then a comprehensive project audit should be carried out
explanation: According to the PMBOK Guide, risk audits can be performed internally or externally. The key is that they follow the approach defined in the project management plan.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112817)

stem: You are developing a risk management plan and need to determine the acceptable level of overall project risk exposure. What should you do first?
options: Determine the risk appetites of key project stakeholders
Consult the risk exposure register within the organizational process assets
Facilitate a brainstorming meeting to develop a comprehensive list of project risks
Develop a set of responses to individual project risks to mitigate overall project risk
explanation: Stakeholder risk appetite defines the acceptable level of risk exposure for the project. Understanding this is a prerequisite to determining acceptable overall project risk.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112819)

stem: After analyzing various factors, your team selects a hybrid life cycle. With the project life cycle selected, how will the product be delivered?
options: As subsets of the overall product
As a single final product at the end of the project
As work packages of the WBS
As product increments based on customer's value
explanation: Hybrid life cycles combine predictive and adaptive elements. Product delivery typically occurs as increments based on customer value, reflecting the adaptive component.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112895)

stem: Due to the progressive elaboration nature of your project, only a few top levels of the WBS have been identified. What is the best approach to determine the team resource requirements?
options: Check the organizational process assets for similar projects and use analogous estimating
Apply bottom-up estimating using the work breakdown structure (WBS) as a guide
Utilize parametric estimating for the individual work packages within the existing WBS
Decompose the WBS down to the work package level during initial project planning
explanation: When only high-level WBS information is available, analogous estimating uses historical data from similar projects to estimate resources, which is appropriate for early-stage planning.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112983)

stem: You are about to begin work on a work package for the braking system of a vehicle and want to review the acceptance criteria before starting. Where can you find this information?
options: Project management plan
Activity attributes
Requirements documentation
The WBS dictionary
explanation: The WBS dictionary contains detailed information about each work package, including acceptance criteria, descriptions, and the responsible party.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112986)

stem: A project manager closes procurements, shares the final project report, updates the lessons learned repository, and obtains feedback from stakeholders. What should the project manager do next?
options: Provide final payment to the suppliers
Send a summary of how the final product achieved the business needs
Transfer the completed assembly line to operations
Send formal written notice to the vendors that the contracts have been completed
explanation: Transferring the product or deliverable to operations is a final step in the Close Project or Phase process after all administrative closure activities are complete.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6112994)

stem: A project team is evaluating two risk responses. The first has two criteria and costs $5,000; the second has four criteria and costs $2,000. What action is the project team LEAST likely to take in selecting the best risk response?
options: Running a Monte Carlo simulation
Performing an alternatives analysis
Conducting a cost-benefit analysis
Using multicriteria decision analysis
explanation: Monte Carlo simulation is used in quantitative risk analysis to model overall project risk, not to select between individual risk responses. The other options are appropriate decision-making tools for this comparison.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113034)

stem: The only qualified scrum master in the company is already assigned to another project and her functional manager won't cooperate. How could this situation have been prevented?
options: The project manager should have checked the project's resource calendars for the availability of the scrum master.
The project sponsor should have directed the project manager to serve as both the project manager and the scrum master.
The scrum master should have refused to work on the other project knowing that she is the only qualified resource in the company.
The availability of the scrum master should have been negotiated between the functional manager and the project manager during project initiation.
explanation: Negotiating critical resource availability early during initiation ensures key resources are secured before planning begins, preventing conflicts during execution.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113042)

stem: Your project team members are writing software code as a pair and reviewing each other's work to determine if the code conforms to the requirements documentation. Which of the following will be a result of these reviews?
options: Accepted deliverable
Acceptance criteria
Work performance data
Verified deliverable
explanation: The output of Control Quality, including code reviews and inspections checking conformance to requirements, is a verified deliverable. Acceptance by the customer produces accepted deliverables.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113060)

stem: An iteration planning meeting is dominated by the most senior developer who frequently interrupts others. The project manager wants to ensure all voices are heard in the next meeting. What is the best course of action?
options: Discuss the issue with the senior developer, and for the next meeting allocate equal amounts of time for each team member to speak
Inform the senior developer's functional manager about this unacceptable behavior and request disciplinary action
Post a copy of the team's social contract in the meeting room and review the contents at the start of the next iteration planning meeting
Invite the sponsor to the next iteration planning meeting to have the senior developer be more thoughtful in the presence of the sponsor
explanation: The team's social contract (working agreement) defines acceptable behaviors. Reviewing it at the meeting start is a constructive, team-empowering way to address behavioral norms without singling anyone out.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113063)

stem: A scrum team will handle the software development phase. The organization is familiar with waterfall but will use scrum. What is the best strategy to ensure all scrum roles and responsibilities are served?
options: Remove the business analyst from the software development phase since that role is only associated with waterfall project management
Mentor the business analyst to serve in the role of the product owner during the software development phase since those roles are closely aligned
Request that one of the programmers serve as the voice-of-the-customer for prioritizing items in the product backlog
Ask the most experienced tester to approve or reject the deliverables during the sprint review
explanation: Business analysts are skilled in requirements elicitation and stakeholder communication, making them well-suited to transition into the product owner role in a scrum context.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113070)

stem: A construction project has many people and specialized teams at the worksite daily. Frequent communication between team leads is critical for safety and efficiency. Which agile ceremony can the project incorporate to increase timely cross-functional communication?
options: Iteration reviews
Risk-based spikes
Retrospectives
Daily standups
explanation: Daily standups are brief synchronization meetings that facilitate quick cross-functional communication and are directly applicable to non-agile environments like construction sites.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113086)

stem: Team members are not getting the information needed during agile ceremonies. What is the best option to confirm that information is received and understood?
options: Validate with the team that the information was captured and facilitate feedback discussions
Spread out the ceremonies over months between each event to ensure comprehension
Mandate that all project team members update the information radiators after each ceremony
Reduce the number of project team members that attend the ceremonies
explanation: The most effective way to confirm understanding is through feedback — validating that information was captured and inviting discussion ensures two-way communication.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113087)

stem: A project is transitioning from hardware development (predictive) to software development (agile). How might the project manager's role change with respect to integration management?
options: Act as a servant leader by determining the relative size of the story points of the items in the product backlog
Delegate the control of the detailed project work planning and product delivery to the project team members
Ensure that the project team members each have specific tasks assigned to them and recorded in a Gantt chart
Strengthen the change management controls to ensure that scope creep is minimized or entirely eliminated
explanation: In adaptive environments, the project manager's role shifts toward facilitating and delegating detailed planning and delivery decisions to the self-managing development team.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113096)

stem: During a sprint retrospective, the team wants to try pair programming to reduce rework. You agree to empower the team and implement the practice. What should you do next?
options: During the upcoming sprint, measure velocity and, if it decreases, discontinue pair programming and reprimand the team members
Invite the project sponsor and other key stakeholders to demonstrate the increased collaboration between the project team members
Submit a change request to update the communications management plan with the new agile practice of pair programming
At the end of the upcoming sprint, revise the burndown chart, evaluate the productivity, and update the lessons learned register
explanation: After adopting a new practice, the team should inspect and adapt — measuring productivity via the burndown chart and capturing findings in the lessons learned register.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113118)

stem: During a daily standup, a developer raises an impediment with software code that could cause a sprint delay. What is the project manager's best course of action?
options: Write the software code for the developer since it is the project manager's responsibility to remove impediments
Submit a change request to update the schedule baseline to reflect the delay caused by the impediment
Move the user authentication feature back into the project backlog and ask the product owner to reprioritize it
Recommend that the scrum team members use swarming as a technique to clear the impediment
explanation: Swarming involves team members collectively focusing on a single impediment to resolve it quickly, which is an effective agile technique for addressing blockers during a sprint.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113123)

stem: Stakeholders' needs are vague and expressed only in general terms, causing concern about excessive changes and rework. What is the project manager's best course of action?
options: Develop a project scope statement and decompose it down to the work package level as part of initial project planning
Request that the project team use an iterative and incremental approach to product development with short feedback loops
Implement a robust change control system to only allow for the most important changes to be incorporated into the product
Generate an activity list from what is currently known and use the Kanban board to break the activities down to their tasks
explanation: When requirements are vague and uncertain, an iterative/incremental approach with short feedback loops allows for progressive refinement and reduces the risk of costly rework.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113125)

stem: A project manager needs to quickly define cross-functional requirements and reconcile stakeholder differences to build consensus. What is the best course of action?
options: Create a questionnaire and send it out to all of the stakeholders to determine the project requirements
Conduct an in-person interview with each of the relevant stakeholders to determine their needs
Facilitate a requirements workshop with all of the relevant stakeholders to develop user stories
Perform a job shadowing exercise with the intended end-users of the existing software
explanation: Requirements workshops bring stakeholders together to collaboratively define requirements and reconcile differences in a single session, making them ideal when speed and consensus are priorities.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113126)

stem: The organization's leadership wants projects to include more agile methodologies. Before mandating any agile methods to be used in a project, what should be done first?
options: Pause current projects to align them to the new agile cadence
Ask employees to vote on who wants to take part in agile projects
Assess organizational culture and readiness for the transformation
Provide agile training to those who will be part of the pilot project team
explanation: Organizational agility requires cultural readiness. Assessing culture and readiness first identifies gaps and informs an appropriate change management strategy before any mandate is issued.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113130)

stem: You are establishing the relative priorities of individual risks. Which of the following tasks is LEAST likely to impact this prioritization?
options: Reviewing the assumption log
Examining the risk register
Researching the stakeholder register
Creating the risk report
explanation: The risk report is an output of qualitative risk analysis, not an input. Creating it comes after prioritization, so it does not impact the prioritization process itself.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113133)

stem: Team members are frequently changing during a long multi-phase project. The project manager is questioning whether to include agile practices. What should the project manager do prior to moving to a hybrid approach?
options: Ensure project finances are secured for the next project phase
Reduce the team size to nine people, plus or minus two
Target a smaller project as a proof of concept to test the feasibility
Evaluate the benefits of incorporating agile practices
explanation: Before adopting hybrid practices, the project manager should evaluate what specific benefits agile elements would bring to the project and whether they address the current challenges.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113135)

stem: Midway into execution, new cybersecurity laws require audits that may come at any time. What is the best way for the project manager and team to address the risk of audits disrupting the project plans?
options: Handle the audits as an unplanned spike
Reschedule the audits to the end of the project
Request that the sponsor removes the audits
Inform stakeholders that the project will miss its objectives
explanation: In agile/hybrid environments, spikes are used to address uncertainty and unplanned work. Treating compliance audits as unplanned spikes allows the team to accommodate them within the iteration framework.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113136)

stem: A plan-driven project has many deliverables to be released at the end of the phase. The project manager wants to ensure deliverables are completed as expected. Which agile practices can the project manager recommend?
options: Definition of ready and iteration reviews
User stories and confidence votes
Definition of done and demonstrations
Backlog refinement and retrospectives
explanation: Definition of Done ensures deliverables meet a clear set of completion criteria, while demonstrations allow stakeholders to validate that deliverables meet expectations.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113138)

stem: A project manager is considering incorporating daily standups into a plan-driven project. Team members are unsure what is expected of them. What can the project manager suggest they do PRIOR to the daily standup?
options: Make sure their work progress is reflected in the burndown chart
Submit the necessary progress reports to the product owner
Estimate work items that will be developed after the daily standup
Resolve any defects that have been found in the previous day
explanation: Before a daily standup, team members should update their task status (e.g., in the burndown chart or task board) so they can accurately report what they completed, what they plan to do, and any impediments.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113139)

stem: The team identifies that information in two tools (original and new agile) is out of sync. This risk was identified earlier in planning. What is the best course of action?
options: Accept the risk since agile values individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Recognize that the risk has now become an issue and implement the planned response
Conduct a probability and impact assessment to determine a risk score
Communicate to the project sponsor that work may be delayed due to clerical errors
explanation: When a previously identified risk has materialized, it becomes an issue. The project manager should implement the risk response that was planned for this scenario.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113142)

stem: Projects in a program use various approaches (plan-driven, agile, hybrid). Teams complain about entering the same data in multiple tools, reducing productivity. What is the best course of action for the program manager?
options: Direct the teams to continue entering the data in the various systems
Determine what tools and data provide the appropriate oversight
Switch all projects in the program to the agile project management approach
Pause all data entry until a valuable solution can be determined
explanation: The program manager should identify which tools and data are necessary for governance and oversight, eliminating redundancy while maintaining required visibility.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113143)

stem: The sponsor wants to pursue project objectives as planned but also accommodate and adjust to new information and changes as the project progresses. Which project management approach should the project manager select?
options: Waterfall
Agile
Hybrid
Phased
explanation: A hybrid approach combines the structure of predictive planning with the adaptability of agile methods, accommodating both stability and flexibility as the sponsor desires.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113144)

stem: Senior leadership mandates retrospectives for all projects, even though the organization uses plan-driven methods. How should the outputs from the retrospectives be stored and managed?
options: Senior leadership determines the location of the outputs and who may access the contents
