The project team stores the outputs in a location that the project manager can control and limit changes
The project team determines a location that is accessible for relevant stakeholders to view and track
The project manager establishes the location and allows the customer to view and make recommendations
explanation: Retrospective outputs should be stored in a transparent, accessible location for relevant stakeholders, supporting a culture of openness and continuous improvement.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113147)

stem: An approved change request transitions a colocated scrum team to a virtual remote environment starting in sprint 6. What will be the best method to understand the impact of this change on the team's productivity?
options: Check the product burndown chart to look for any changes to the trendline
Calculate the CPI at the end of each sprint to determine the change in the team's productivity
Review the product burnup chart to compare velocity before and after the change
Update the project schedule network diagram to reflect the changes in the team's environment
explanation: The product burnup chart tracks completed work over time and reflects velocity. Comparing velocity before and after the environment change directly shows the productivity impact.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113155)

stem: During a team chartering brainstorming session, the team identifies 37 working agreements. What should the project manager do next?
options: Submit a change request to revise the resource management plan to incorporate the team's working agreements
Verify that all of the project team members support the working agreements and document the agreements in the project charter
Review the team's working agreements at the start of every daily standup and during the sprint review meetings
Ask the project team members for their top five to ten agreements and document the information in the team charter
explanation: A team charter should capture a focused, practical set of working agreements. Narrowing 37 items to the top 5-10 ensures the charter is actionable and memorable.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113157)

stem: A project manager is struggling to lead the team effectively, with unhealthy tension and outbursts during sprint retrospectives. What should the project manager do first?
options: Submit a change request to revise the project management plan to eliminate the requirement for retrospectives
Take a proactive step in improving his own emotional intelligence (EI) to become a more effective leader
Reduce the story size by splitting user stories and use relative estimation with the entire team to estimate
Lead a training session with the project team members to help them become emotionally competent
explanation: The project manager's outbursts are the root cause of the team dysfunction. Improving emotional intelligence is the first and most impactful action the project manager should take.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113159)

stem: A project is three months behind schedule because key stakeholders were resistant to consequential changes. What might have been done differently during project planning to avoid this situation?
options: Better execution of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process
Development of a stakeholder engagement assessment matrix
Creation of a comprehensive stakeholder register
Establishment of a more robust communications management plan
explanation: A stakeholder engagement assessment matrix maps current vs. desired engagement levels, enabling proactive planning to move resistant stakeholders toward supportive engagement.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113216)

stem: Two team members are arguing about whether work packages in the WBS should be arranged in the order the work is carried out. What is the project manager's best response?
options: Work is decomposed and sequenced in the Define Activities process.
Any approach is good as long as the team members avoid the conflict.
The chronological order of tasks is defined in the Sequence Activities process.
The approach should be defined by the project management office (PMO).
explanation: The WBS is not sequenced. Sequencing of work (defining logical relationships between activities) is done in the Sequence Activities process, not in the WBS creation.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113623)

stem: You ordered custom-built manufacturing equipment, secured permission to install a solar array for testing, and reserved a conference room for daily stand-ups. What is the result of your efforts?
options: Resource histogram
Resource management plan
Resource assignment matrix
Physical resource assignments
explanation: Physical resource assignments document the physical resources (equipment, facilities, materials) that have been acquired and assigned to the project.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113718)

stem: During project execution, several unexpected issues are threatening the project schedule and budget. What is the best course of action for the project manager?
options: Use contingency reserves
Review project funding requirements
Use management reserves
Review organizational process assets
explanation: Contingency reserves are allocated to handle identified risks and known unknowns that may arise during execution. Unexpected issues that were part of identified risk scenarios are managed with contingency reserves.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113733)

stem: The project will procure goods and services from outside the organization. The project manager instructs the team to develop a comprehensive list of individual project risks related to those procurements. What is the best course of action?
options: Consult the project charter
Review procurement documentation
Develop a procurement register
Check the probability and impact matrix
explanation: Procurement documentation is a key input to Identify Risks and contains information about contracts and supplier arrangements that may introduce specific risks to the project.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113755)

stem: You are managing a house renovation with a budget of $100,000. You have completed 40% of the work and spent $60,000. How much more will it cost to complete the project if the cost performance remains unchanged?
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6113796)

stem: A major dispute arises with a vendor. The project manager claims the vendor failed contractual obligations; the vendor complains about late payments. What is the best course of action?
options: Refer this issue to a third party for mediation or arbitration
File a contract claim and then work directly with the vendor to resolve the issue
Terminate the contract, document lessons learned, and find another vendor
Conduct a procurement audit
explanation: When disputes arise, the preferred approach is to file a formal claim and attempt to resolve it directly with the vendor before escalating to third-party arbitration.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6125212)

stem: You want to recognize the contributions of high-performing team members. Which of the following do you need to guide you through this process?
options: Organizational process assets
Stakeholder register
Project team assignments
Quality metrics
explanation: Organizational process assets include reward and recognition systems and policies that guide how team members are formally recognized and rewarded for their contributions.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6125216)

stem: You are managing a highly regulated bridge construction project and developing a plan to establish criteria and activities for developing, monitoring, and controlling the schedule. Which of the following should you gather to complete this process?
options: The charter, project management plan, estimation databases, and PMO templates
Government standards, the assumption log, schedule management plan, and milestone list
The resource breakdown structure, lessons learned register, and scheduling methodology
The project scope statement, risk register, and project team assignments
explanation: The Plan Schedule Management process uses the project charter, project management plan, enterprise environmental factors (including estimation databases), and organizational process assets (including PMO templates) as inputs.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6150715)

stem: One project activity requires searching a large area to find debris from a failed prototype explosion. There is debate about what type and quantity of resources are needed. What should the project manager do first?
options: Select any resource and quantity to complete the task by the due date
Request the team to vote and make a decision based on majority
Research various levels of resource capability and skills, types, and sizes
Utilize a PMIS to manage resource pools and allocate resources
explanation: Before making resource decisions, the project manager should research the different types, capabilities, and quantities of resources available to make an informed estimate.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151376)

stem: A project manager is developing a cost management plan. The cost of capital is 9.7% (equity), 6.7% (debt), and 5.1% (self-funding). The NPV is $500,000 and the opportunity cost is $750,000. What is the project manager's best course of action?
options: Fund the project with equity since there are no dividend obligations
Select the self-funding option since it provides the lowest cost of capital
Perform an alternatives analysis since there are multiple factors to consider
Recommend the termination of the project since another project has a higher NPV
explanation: With multiple factors at play (cost of capital, NPV, opportunity cost, legal mandate), the project manager should conduct an alternatives analysis to evaluate the best funding option comprehensively.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151382)

stem: You are estimating monetary resources for a large-scale multinational project lasting at least seven years. Your previous projects were all domestic with short timeframes. What might you need to do differently compared to past projects?
options: Create a stakeholder engagement assessment matrix
Develop a more robust risk management plan
Consider additional enterprise environmental factors
Include additional organizational process assets
explanation: A multinational, long-duration project introduces unique enterprise environmental factors such as currency exchange rates, inflation, international regulations, and cultural differences that must be factored into cost estimation.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151383)

stem: During a product review, a client detects a defect and rejects one of the deliverables, even though the deliverables were previously verified. What should you do?
options: Submit a change request to fix the deliverable
Refund the customer for the value of the deliverable
Ask the project team to repair the defect
Begin the claims administration process
explanation: When a deliverable is rejected during Validate Scope, the appropriate response is to submit a change request (defect repair) through the formal change control process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151389)

stem: Not all team members are properly checking out resources used in the project. What is the best option for the project manager?
options: Wait until there is a need or the team is impacted
Escalate the issue to the functional managers
Discuss the checkout rationale with the team
Remove the process of checking out resources
explanation: Discussing the rationale with the team addresses the root cause of non-compliance by helping team members understand why the process matters, which is more constructive than escalation or elimination of the process.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151393)

stem: In a pharmaceutical project, the CEO announces that the patient pool should double and that results should be tracked in a new system launching this week. What should the project manager do first?
options: Ramp up production to match the demand of the CEO
Consider what might be included in a change request
Adjust the scope baseline to reflect the increased trial size
Communicate the change in the scope to the project stakeholders
explanation: The CEO's announcement is a proposed scope change. The project manager must first consider what should be included in a formal change request before any action can be taken.
source: PM PrepCast – Free PMP Practice Exam (Qid 6151395)
